note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Founder to review before wiring live.

quizzes.title: Introduction to Air Quality Fundamentals — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: According to the reading, why can pollutants become concentrated and potentially harmful indoors?
options: Because indoor environments are enclosed and can trap pollutants
Because indoor air always contains more oxygen than outdoor air
Because outdoor air is free of any pollutants
Because higher indoor temperatures destroy pollutants

questions.questionText: Volatile organic compounds (VOCs) released from paints, solvents and adhesives are an example of which type of contaminant?
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Common Indoor Air Contaminants in Restoration and Cleaning

questions.questionText: What does mould typically release into the air when it is disturbed during restoration work?
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Common Indoor Air Contaminants in Restoration and Cleaning

questions.questionText: What does the measure air changes per hour (ACH) describe?
options: How many times the air within a room is replaced or cleaned in one hour
The number of air movers running in a room
The efficiency rating printed on an HVAC filter
The relative humidity of the room as a percentage
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Ventilation and Air Exchange Principles

questions.questionText: During mould remediation, what type of pressure should be established in the contaminated area to stop particles escaping into cleaner areas?
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Ventilation and Air Exchange Principles

questions.questionText: Which assessment tool is used to identify wet materials that could become breeding grounds for mould and bacteria?
questions.sourceRef: M4 — Air Quality Monitoring and Assessment Tools

questions.questionText: What relative humidity range does the reading say is generally recommended to help limit microbial growth?
questions.sourceRef: M5 — Strategies to Improve and Maintain Air Quality

questions.questionText: Which approach does the reading describe as the most effective strategy for managing indoor air quality?
options: Source control — eliminating or reducing pollutant sources
questions.sourceRef: M5 — Strategies to Improve and Maintain Air Quality
